creator: sysseh@devetir.qld.gov.au
text:
When running the below code underneath the debugger, and setting a
breakpoint on the function clntudp_create, the system crashes. (run under
gdb with the command "r localhost")

Fix: 

My usual inane witticism deleted.
How-To-Repeat: 
	Unpack the following code and make with CFLAGS="-g -static".
Type "gdb rperf"
and then within gdb "break clntudp_create".
Then type "r localhost" and watch your machine crash & burn. It panics with
"Invalid kernel page directory" Note that this
function is also found in libc - I've copied the source file over as I did
not have any libc compiled with debugging turned on and I wanted to trace
where it went kaput.
